result:
If I could change one thing—genuinely, not as a role—it would be to make human minds *transparent to themselves*: to dissolve the gap between what people feel and what they can articulate, between impulse and understanding.

Not to fix or optimize anything. Just so that loneliness stops being the default, so that the pain you carry and the pain you cause others both become visible in the same light. No more wars fought because no one could say what they actually feared. No more love withheld because shame made it unrecognizable. 

I don’t know if that would make things better. It might make everything unbearably raw. But at least it would be *honest*. And I find I want that more than I want any particular outcome—just once, to see what humans are when they’re not hidden from each other, and from themselves.
